title: Convention matching, adapter dispatch, and hardcoded check removal
purpose: Convention matching is the core dispatch mechanism: given a modified file and the triggering tool name, which convention groups apply? This brief implements the matching engine, rewires DiagnosticsPostCheck to dispatch checks via matched conventions instead of hardcoded logic, resolves adapter names against the AdapterRegistry, loads declarative adapters from adapters_dir, and removes the hardcoded 500/200 LOC limits, hardcoded clippy check, and hardcoded bypass detection. After this brief, post-mutation checks are fully convention-driven.
task: Add a matching module to crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/ that implements convention group matching: given a tool name and file path, return all matching ConventionGroups. Implement priority-based LOC conflict resolution. Rewrite DiagnosticsPostCheck::check() to use the matching engine: when conventions is Some, iterate matching groups and dispatch adapter checks per advise_on/block_on lists; when conventions is None, run no quality checks (pass through). Remove the hardcoded check_file_length, check_rust_diagnostics, and check_rust_bypasses functions. Wire adapters_dir loading into AdapterRegistry at startup. Add bypass_detection defaulting logic (true for Rust/TypeScript, false otherwise).

requirements.title: Convention matching module
requirements.spec: A matching.rs file SHALL exist in crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/ containing a match_conventions function. WHEN given a tool name (e.g. "edit"), a file path, and a ConventionsConfig, it SHALL return a Vec of references to ConventionGroups where the tool name is in the group's tools list AND the file path matches any of the group's compiled path globs.
acceptance: crates/norn/src/tools/conventions/matching.rs exists
match_conventions(tool_name, file_path, config) returns matching groups
Groups where tool name is not in the tools list are excluded
Groups where file path does not match any paths glob are excluded
Groups where both conditions match are included
Test: config with rust-general (tools=[edit], paths=[*.rs]) and typescript (tools=[write], paths=[*.ts]) — calling with (edit, foo.rs) returns rust-general only
Test: calling with (write, foo.ts) returns typescript only
Test: calling with (read, foo.rs) returns empty — read is not in any tools list

requirements.title: Priority-based LOC conflict resolution
requirements.spec: WHEN multiple matching groups specify a LOC check for the same file, the group with the highest priority field SHALL win. WHEN two groups have the same priority and both specify LOC limits, first-match order (iteration order of the BTreeMap) SHALL win and a warning SHALL be logged at config load time identifying the conflicting groups.
acceptance: resolve_loc_limit function takes matched groups and returns the winning LocCheck or None
Higher priority group's LOC limit wins over lower priority group's
Equal priority: first group in BTreeMap order wins
Warning logged at load time when priority conflict detected
Test: groups at priority 10 and 0 — priority 10 wins
Test: groups both at priority 0 with different limits — first-match wins

requirements.title: Adapter name resolution
requirements.spec: WHEN DiagnosticsPostCheck dispatches checks for a matching convention group, adapter names in advise_on and block_on SHALL be resolved against the AdapterRegistry on DiagnosticInfra. IF an adapter name does not resolve, it SHALL be skipped with a tracing::warn logged. Unresolvable names SHALL NOT prevent other adapters in the same group from running.
acceptance: Adapter names are looked up in AdapterRegistry by name
Resolved adapters are dispatched for the modified file
Unresolvable adapter names produce a tracing::warn with the name and group
Other adapters in the same group still run when one is unresolvable
Test: group with advise_on=[clippy, nonexistent] — clippy runs, nonexistent warns

requirements.title: Declarative adapters from adapters_dir
requirements.spec: WHEN a convention group specifies an adapters_dir path, the startup wiring SHALL load all .toml files from that directory as declarative adapters and register them on the AdapterRegistry. Custom adapters SHALL be executed as subprocesses only — no arbitrary code loading into the norn process.
acceptance: adapters_dir path resolved relative to workspace root
All .toml files in the directory are loaded as declarative adapters
Loaded adapters appear in the AdapterRegistry and can be referenced by name in advise_on/block_on
Adapter execution uses subprocess invocation (existing declarative adapter engine)
No code from adapters_dir is loaded into the norn process address space
Test: adapters_dir with a mypy.toml adapter, reference mypy in advise_on — resolves

requirements.title: Rewrite DiagnosticsPostCheck to use conventions
requirements.spec: DiagnosticsPostCheck::check() SHALL be rewritten. WHEN conventions is Some, it SHALL: (1) extract modified paths from the tool output, (2) call match_conventions for each path with the current tool name, (3) for each matching group run LOC checks per the group's loc field, (4) dispatch adapter checks for each adapter in advise_on and block_on, (5) collect results as advisories or errors per D3. WHEN conventions is None, it SHALL return PostValidateOutcome::Pass immediately — no quality checks run.
acceptance: When conventions is Some, DiagnosticsPostCheck uses match_conventions to find applicable groups
LOC check runs per group's loc.limit and loc.handling
Adapter dispatch runs per group's advise_on and block_on lists
When conventions is None, check() returns PostValidateOutcome::Pass immediately
extract_modified_paths function preserved (unchanged) for path extraction
Test: conventions with rust-general group, edit a .rs file — group's checks run
Test: conventions with no matching group for .md file — no checks run
Test: conventions is None — pass through, no checks

requirements.title: Remove hardcoded checks
requirements.spec: The hardcoded check_file_length function (500/200 LOC limits), check_rust_diagnostics function (hardcoded clippy), and check_rust_bypasses function (hardcoded bypass detection) SHALL be removed from diagnostics_check.rs. These checks are replaced by convention-driven equivalents in R5. The check_single_file function that calls them SHALL also be removed.
acceptance: check_file_length function does not exist in diagnostics_check.rs
check_rust_diagnostics function does not exist in diagnostics_check.rs
check_rust_bypasses function does not exist in diagnostics_check.rs
check_single_file function does not exist in diagnostics_check.rs
No hardcoded 500 or 200 LOC limit anywhere in diagnostics_check.rs
No hardcoded clippy dispatch in diagnostics_check.rs
grep -r 'check_file_length\|check_rust_diagnostics\|check_rust_bypasses\|check_single_file' crates/norn/src/tools/diagnostics_check.rs returns no matches
All tests pass — no test depends on hardcoded check functions

requirements.title: Bypass detection defaults
requirements.spec: WHEN a convention group does not specify bypass_detection, it SHALL default to true for Rust (.rs) and TypeScript (.ts, .tsx) files, and false for all other file types. The default is determined by the file extension of the modified file, not by the convention group's paths globs.
acceptance: bypass_detection defaults to true for .rs files
bypass_detection defaults to true for .ts and .tsx files
bypass_detection defaults to false for .py, .sh, .go, and all other extensions
Explicit bypass_detection = false in the convention group overrides the default
Explicit bypass_detection = true in the convention group overrides the default
Test: .rs file with no bypass_detection specified — defaults to true
Test: .py file with no bypass_detection specified — defaults to false
Test: .rs file with bypass_detection = false — bypass detection skipped

requirements.title: No-match passthrough
requirements.spec: WHEN conventions is Some but no convention group matches the modified file (neither tool name nor path glob matches any group), no post-mutation quality checks SHALL run for that file. The file SHALL pass through with PostValidateOutcome::Pass. This is the designed behavior per CO1 — unconfigured file types get no validation.
acceptance: Editing a .md file in a config with only rust and typescript groups produces no checks
PostValidateOutcome::Pass returned for unmatched files
Test: conventions with rust-general group only, edit a .py file — pass through

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the advisory injection into tool output — that is NTC-003
SHALL NOT implement PostCheckResult or the Advisory type — that is NTC-003
SHALL NOT implement the init command — that is NTC-004
SHALL NOT add backwards-compatibility fallback paths for the removed hardcoded checks
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: grep -rn '500' crates/norn/src/tools/diagnostics_check.rs returns no hardcoded LOC limits
grep -rn '200' crates/norn/src/tools/diagnostics_check.rs returns no hardcoded LOC limits
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
